reasoning: Item 19 reports historical AUV data for 2,219 franchised Traditional Restaurants operating for the full 2024 fiscal year. The standard royalty rate is 4% of Gross Sales for Traditional Restaurants. The reporting population consists of restaurants that have been operating for an average of 25 years, meaning nearly all are paying the standard 4% royalty rate. New franchisees under the NRO incentive program pay reduced royalties (0.5%-2%) for the first 5 years, but these NRO restaurants are a small minority of the 2,219-unit population and their lower royalty rate would, if anything, slightly overstate profitability relative to what a mature unit at 4% would show (since the Item 19 is revenue-only and does not reflect royalty costs). Because Item 19 is revenue-only (AUV = Gross Sales), the royalty rate does not affect the reported AUV figures at all — the same sales figure is reported regardless of whether the franchisee pays 0.5% or 4% royalty. Therefore, there is no cohort comparability issue.
note: Item 19 is revenue-only. Royalty rate does not affect AUV reporting. NRO incentive ramp applies to new restaurants but does not create a comparability gap in the AUV data.
